proposed_relations.description: BRT stations with bus bulbs along the Nostrand/Rogers SBS route, from DeKalb to Church Aves

evidence_refs.source_quote: bus bulbs at stations from DeKalb to Church Aves

proposed_relations.description: BRT stations with bus bulbs along Nostrand Avenue from DeKalb to Church Aves

evidence_refs.source_quote: bus bulbs at stations from DeKalb to Church Aves

rationale: p008_c0003 explicitly mentions 'bus bulbs at stations from DeKalb to Church Aves' as part of the Nostrand/Rogers SBS project features. While p005_c0012 is only the 'Stations' caption label, p008_c0003 provides project-level evidence that stations with bus bulbs are a treatment on this corridor.

evidence_refs.source_quote: bus bulbs at stations from DeKalb to Church Aves

quarantine.note: The cited blocks do not establish that BRT stations are an independent treatment of the project or corridor. p005_c0012 ('Stations') is a single-word slide caption/label with no relational content whatsoever — it does not state any connection between stations and the project or corridor. p008_c0003 ('Offset bus lanes for 5 miles, with bus bulbs at stations from DeKalb to Church Aves') mentions stations only as sub-locations for bus bulbs within the offset bus lanes treatment; the block text describes offset bus lanes as the treatment, not stations. The phrase 'bus bulbs at stations' treats stations as incidental location markers, not as a standalone treatment component. Neither block states that the project or corridor 'has' a stations treatment. The same block p008_c0003 is already correctly used to establish the offset-bus-lanes treatment relation; extracting a second, independent stations relation from the same incidental mention constitutes double-counting without distinct relational evidence.
